title: CVE-2019-14271 loading of nsswitch based config inside chroot under Glibc
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

   When I run `buildah bud` with the following Dockerfile, I get an
error from `buildah-copier` about glibc:

```
FROM golang:1.13.1 AS build
FROM centos:centos7
COPY --from=build /etc/os-release ./
```

```
$ sudo buildah --version
buildah version 1.16.4 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)

$ sudo buildah bud -f Dockerfile -t localhost/test
STEP 1: FROM golang:1.13.1 AS build
Getting image source signatures
Copying config 52b59e9ead done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 52b59e9ead8
STEP 2: FROM centos:centos7
STEP 3: COPY --from=build /etc/os-release ./
error building at STEP "COPY --from=build /etc/os-release ./": error
adding sources [/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/a0b4ebe4fdf83ff427d12396dcdfe3eb36dae872b32d293b73c7f508fd0b4b01/merged/etc/os-release]:
error reading "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/a0b4ebe4fdf83ff427d12396dcdfe3eb36dae872b32d293b73c7f508fd0b4b01/merged/etc/os-release":
buildah-copier: relocation error:
/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libnss_files.so.2: symbol
__libc_readline_unlocked, version GLIBC_PRIVATE not defined in file
libc.so.6 with link time reference
```

The only reason I encountered this is because I
am building this code on a CentOS 7 machine and just moved up to a
newer version of buildah (anything beyond 1.16.0) which has this
issue.  From what I can tell Go is loading the NSS libraries which
then includes the glibc.  However on CentOS 7 with the golang
container there is a mismatch which causes a linker error.

   After doing a bunch of digging it appeared this issue is related to
the CVE above, and pulling this fix into the checked out buildah code,
recompiling, and testing solves the issue:

https://github.com/moby/moby/pull/39612/files

  Since this appears to be related to a CVE this should probably be
included as soon as possible.  Also, this prevents me from compiling
Go code with the upstream golang containers using a newer version of
buildah on any of the EL-7 based OS's.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. See Dockerfile and commands above

**Describe the results you received:**
Failed to pull in the built Go binary into my build container:
buildah-copier: relocation error: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libnss_files.so.2: symbol __libc_readline_unlocked, version GLIBC_PRIVATE not defined in file libc.so.6 with link time reference

**Describe the results you expected:**
For it to properly pull in the binary

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
This happens with the buildah I built myself since this is not available on CentOS7.
It happens with any version of buildah past 1.16.0, tested with 1.16.0, 1.16.4 and 1.16.5
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version 1.16.4 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
I am running this on CentOS7 so it is using docker
docker --version
Docker version 1.13.1, build 64e9980/1.13.1
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
CentOS Linux release 7.8.2003 (Core)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux build-centos7 3.10.0-1127.18.2.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Sun Jul 26 15:27:06 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
It is the default storage.conf and does not affect this outcome
```
